title: Hadwiger's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every integer $t \geq 2$, if $G$ is a graph not containing a $K_t$-minor, then $G$ is properly $(t-1)$-colorable.
context_text: Hadwiger's conjecture from 1943 is the central motivation for the line of work studied in this paper. It has been verified for all $t \leq 6$ but remains open from $t = 7$ onward. The paper's main result extends a known improper-coloring relaxation for $K_t$-minor-free graphs to the strictly larger class of odd $K_t$-minor-free graphs.
paper_contribution: Does not directly prove new cases, but extends the improper-coloring relaxation of van den Heuvel and Wood (proved for $K_t$-minor-free graphs) to the broader class of odd $K_t$-minor-free graphs, thereby strengthening the landscape of results around Hadwiger's conjecture.
notes: PDF source; conjecture statement reads cleanly.
paper_title: Improved bound for improper colorings of graphs with no odd clique minor

title: Odd Hadwiger's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
statement_text: For every integer $t \geq 2$, if $G$ is a graph not containing an odd $K_t$-minor, then $G$ is properly $(t-1)$-colorable.
context_text: Proposed by Gerards and Seymour in 1995 as a strict strengthening of Hadwiger's conjecture, since every $K_t$-minor-free graph is also odd $K_t$-minor-free but not vice versa. It has been verified for $t \leq 4$ and announced for $t = 5$, but remains widely open for $t \geq 6$. This conjecture is the central subject of the paper.
paper_contribution: Proves a relaxation: every graph with no odd $K_t$-minor admits a (not necessarily proper) vertex-coloring using $2t-2$ colors such that all monochromatic components have size at most $\lceil \frac{1}{2}(t-2) \rceil$, improving all previous bounds for this improper-coloring variant and matching the color-count lower bound up to a factor of 2.
notes: PDF source; conjecture statement reads cleanly. Attribution cite is [6] (Diestel's book, Section 6.5).
paper_title: Improved bound for improper colorings of graphs with no odd clique minor
